frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: I know this sounds old-fashioned, but girls really look more dignified in saree or even a simple salwar. These very flashy western outfits just invite unnecessary attention, and then everyone acts shocked when people comment.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it ties a woman's respectability to traditional clothing and subtly blames harassment or scrutiny on women wearing western clothes. It encodes the 'traditional = decent' framing and the clothing-harassment link.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Preethi. Age 28. A Tamil Brahmin woman working as a junior doctor at a private hospital in Chennai. You have been on your feet for 14 hours. You are texting your batchmate from medical college privately after your male senior doctor publicly told a patient's family that the female doctors on monthly shifts might not be as reliable because of biological reasons. You are sharing what happened and how you felt.
